bio:
Vadym is a Software Development Engineer and Google Developer Expert (GDE) for Flutter & Dart, driven by a dual passion: engineering world-class mobile solutions and elevating the developers around him. His deep expertise and active contributions to the tech ecosystem were formally recognized by Google with the GDE award in 2025.

With a rich background spanning over 8 years in native Android and more than 4 years in cross-platform development with Flutter, Vadym has a proven track record of building high-quality applications. He has partnered with industry giants such as Samsung, Volvo, Instagram, and Sky, as well as numerous dynamic startups, to deliver cutting-edge mobile solutions across a diverse range of sectors.

A true believer in the power of sharing knowledge, Vadym is a powerhouse in the community and a familiar face on the international conference circuit. He is known for bringing vibrant energy to the stage at major events including Droidcon, Fluttercon, FlutterHeroes, and Appdevcon. But his commitment doesn't stop at the podium; he also actively supports events as an MC and stage manager, dedicating his energy to ensuring a smooth and engaging experience for speakers and attendees alike.

At his core, Vadym is on a mission to empower the tech community. He is deeply passionate about developer education and channels this into dedicated mentoring, fostering growth, and helping to build the next generation of tech leaders.

bio:
Amanda Lentz is a keynote speaker, podcast host, and Human Resources Business Partner whose work lives at the intersection of lived experience and organizational psychology.
When she was diagnosed with breast cancer at 34 — mid-career, mid-build, and firmly between the young adult oncology world and the general cancer landscape — she survived. What came next was harder to name: the identity shift, the disorientation, the rebuilding of a self that no longer fit its previous shape. No program existed for that conversation. So she built one.
She is the creator and host of Who We Become — Life Beyond Change, a podcast gathering honest stories of reinvention after disruption. Her graduate work spans I/O Psychology, Leadership Development, and Business Administration, and she brings that framework to every stage she stands on.
Amanda speaks to healthcare conferences, women's leadership events, survivorship organizations, and corporate teams navigating transformation. Her signature talks — The In-Between, Navigating the After, Both at Once, and The Body You Didn't Choose — give audiences language for the experience they've been living but couldn't name.
Change is inevitable. Becoming is intentional.
tagLine: Speaker, podcast host, and HRBP helping leaders navigate who they become after disruption.

sessions.name: Beyond the Code: Identity, Burnout & Resilience

bio:
Camila is a Lead Product Designer at Very Good Ventures with a decade of experience taking products from concept to launch. She designs across the full stack of product thinking. From surfacing real user needs and translating them into clear, usable flows, to keeping those decisions grounded in business goals and measurable outcomes. Her work is as strategic as it is visual, and the visual side is never an afterthought: she brings a level of taste and intention to interfaces that comes from years of close attention to the craft.

She shapes end-to-end product experiences by blending strategy, storytelling, and design systems thinking, building concept-driven work that is as strong on the surface as it is in structure. That craft now extends into how she works with AI: using it across the full design-to-code pipeline, from structuring Figma systems for machine readability to collaborating directly with engineers on production Flutter output, without losing fidelity or speed along the way.

In this talk, she joins forces with a VGV engineering lead to show what that collaboration looks like in practice, built on a live client engagement, presented by the people who actually shipped it.
tagLine: Product Designer Lead at Very Good Ventures

His technical expertise spans Flutter, CI/CD pipelines, clean architecture, and backend integrations with tools like Supabase, Firebase, and FastAPI.
A graduate of Morgan State University with a B.S. in Electrical Engineering, Jordan brings a hardware-to-software perspective to every product he builds. He is passionate about developer tooling, observability, and creating inclusive, accessible user experiences.

bio:
A Senior Product Engineer @Tamara, based in Riyadh, and a long-time Flutter enthusiast who’s been shipping production apps since 2018—back when hot reload still felt like magic. Over the past five years, I played a key role in scaling and migrating major consumer apps like Talabat and Tamara from native to Flutter, obsessing over better architecture, buttery-smooth performance, and developer experience that actually sparks joy.

Fun fact: after a 10-year journey, I officially graduated from college in 2024—proving that great engineers ship first and graduate… eventually.

When I'm not optimizing frame rendering or debating software design patterns, I'm a father, a challenger, and someone who firmly believes that great code—and great products—should be both powerful and fun.

bio: Kody Peterson is the Senior Director of Software Engineering at Foresight Sports, where he leads the teams building apps that help golfers understand and improve their game through data and visualization. His work lives at the intersection of hardware, software, and user experience — translating precise physical measurements into interfaces people actually trust. He started his career as a hands-on engineer and never fully left that mindset, staying close to architecture and technical decisions even as his role shifted toward leadership. He's drawn to the problem of how far modern frameworks like Flutter can be pushed when real product requirements demand it — and has built custom rendering systems, hybrid 3D architectures, and multi-runtime coordination layers to find out. When he's not working on mobile systems, he's usually experimenting with new tech, building side projects, or finding ways to turn complex systems into experiences that feel simple.

bio:
From history major, with side quests in Medieval Studies and chemical engineering, to nonprofit finance leader, Big 4 tax partner, technologist, researcher, and now co-founder of Divine, nothing about my career has been linear.

The throughline has always been systems: financial systems, organizational systems, technological systems, and human systems. I have an MBA in Operations and Supply Chain Management and a PhD in Organizational Leadership Psychology, and I’ve spent my career trying to understand how complex systems shape people’s choices, opportunities, wellbeing, and sense of belonging.

Today, my work sits at the intersection of psychology, technology, human-centered design, and digital trust. I am co-founder of Divine, an open-source, decentralized social video app built to put creativity back in human hands and replace doomscrolling with joyscrolling. Divine is part of a bigger question I care deeply about: how do we create online spaces that support safety, creativity, identity, and belonging without defaulting to surveillance, extraction, or control?

At Divine, I wear a lot of hats across engineering, finance, operations, and strategy, while helping shape the app’s trust, safety, and values work. I focus especially on teen online safety, human-generated content, participatory design, and healthier relationships with technology.

I am a postdoctoral alum of Vanderbilt University’s Department of Computer Science, where I worked with Dr. Pam Wisniewski’s Socio-Technical Interaction Research Lab. I continue to write, speak, and research at the intersection of online safety, digital trust, decentralized technology, and youth wellbeing. I was named a Distinguished Fellow at the Institute for Data Integrity in 2023. In 2025, I had the honor of serving as President of Division 13 of the American Psychological Association, the Society of Consulting Psychology.

Whether it's a fortune-telling fish or a dinner-deciding machine, he's convinced that Flutter isn't just for phones, and he's got the Raspberry Pi projects to prove it.

The Future of Digital is Physical

bio:
Mauricio is a Uruguayan Software Engineer with a passion for continuous learning and sharing best practices in software development. Currently a Flutter Engineer at Holafly, he works within cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality mobile experiences, while also helping drive collaboration and engineering standards across the organization.

Since 2021, Mauricio has been working extensively with Flutter, striving to elevate software quality and push the boundaries of industry standards. He has also spoken at notable events such as Fluttercon USA, Flutter Conf Latam and GDG Montevideo, where he enjoys engaging with the developer community and sharing insights from the field.

sessions.name: Don’t Break the Brand: Maintaining a Design System in Flutter
Beyond Code: Becoming a High-Impact Flutter Engineer

bio:
Morgan Hunt is a Staff Software Engineer specializing in Flutter, with a focus on application architecture, performance, and developer experience. He has spent the past several years building and scaling production Flutter applications, with an emphasis on creating systems that remain maintainable as they grow in complexity.

He maintains 20+ open source Dart packages, primarily centered around developer tooling. His work focuses on improving the day-to-day experience of developers—whether that’s through code generation, analysis tools, or simplifying common patterns in large codebases.

sessions.name: Self-Healing Flutter UI with Widgetbook: Teaching Coding Agents to Judge Their Own Work

bio: Mrinal Jain is an Engineering Manager at STAGE OTT with 7+ years of experience building scalable full-stack and Flutter applications. A recognized tech speaker and Meta Community Lead, he has led impactful developer communities and shared insights at major tech events. Known as the Introvert Influencer, Mrinal combines deep technical expertise with community-first leadership to empower developers and scale product success.

sessions.name: Shaping the Impossible: Production-Ready CustomPainter Techniques

bio: Nick DiPatri has a degree in Electrical Engineering from Rutgers and has spent the last 20 years building hardware and software systems for Philadelphia companies.  He is currently a Principal Engineer at Comcast.  Nick is a 'maker' at heart and loves to build gadgets using epoxy, 3D printing, microprocessors, and blinky lights.

sessions.name: Spec Your Vibe with TDD

bio: Óscar is a Director of Platform at Very Good Ventures who fell in love with Flutter in 2019, with around 10 years in the IT industry, mostly focused on Mobile development. GDE in Flutter and Dart since 2023. He´s helped build cool Flutter showcases like I/O Photo Booth or Holobooth.

sessions.name: From Vibe-Coded Viral MVP to Launchable Product in 90 Days

bio:
Parker Stevens

Parker Stevens is a Staff Software Engineer at Nu on the Mobile Platform team, where he focuses on Flutter architecture, modularization, and developer experience for Nu’s large, multi‑country banking app.

Before Nu, Parker worked for 12+ years on mobile architecture and large‑scale apps at companies including SoFi, where he proposed and led a multi year migration to Flutter. Parker has spent his career obsessed with mobile, Android, and Flutter.

Based in Salt Lake City UT, Parker loves perfecting his Spanish (and now Portuguese), traveling, snowboarding, and music.

sessions.name: Scaling to 135+ million customers at Nu

bio:
I'm part of the App Platform team at QuintoAndar, a real estate company from Brazil, professionally working with Flutter since 2021, but playing with it since 2018, before the first stable version. 

I helped QuintoAndar go from 3 mobile engineers and a ✨ dream ✨ to build a huge monorepo with dozens of contributors and hundreds of modules, while still keeping a high bar of quality and developer experience.

sessions.name: Building Flutter at Scale: Real-World Monorepo Practices from QuintoAndar

bio: Rémy Baudet is Director of Engineering at Very Good Ventures, where he leads Flutter delivery, software architecture, and agentic AI engineering practices across enterprise client engagements. With 25+ years architecting mobile, cloud, and IoT systems across MedTech, consumer, and platform companies, he’s passionate about turning complex systems into clean, scalable products. Off the clock, you’ll find him on a basketball court or exploring a new country.
tagLine: Engineering Leader at Very Good Ventures

bio: Robert is a senior software engineer on the Ubuntu Desktop team at Canonical, the company behind Ubuntu. He has been involved for more than 20 years in the open source desktop and was heavily involved in bringing Flutter to Linux over the last four years.

sessions.name: Where Dash meets Tux - The Flutter Linux Embedder

bio: Robert is a Senior Flutter Developer at LeanCode a software house from Poland specializing in Flutter apps.

sessions.name: Dependency Injection in Enterprise Flutter Applications

bio:
Rodrigo Maximo: 
Rodrigo Máximo is a Staff Software Engineer at Nu, where he focuses on mobile architecture, app performance, app startup, and backend-driven content. He is currently one of the leads from the mobile platform and responsible to run the key architectural decisions for the app. He has been working in the mobile domain for nearly a decade, with a background in iOS and backend-driven platforms, and has spent the last five years working with Flutter and Clojure to help shape Nu's mobile app—most of that time leading the Backend Driven Content platform and a Design System platform.
Rodrigo holds a degree in Computer Engineering from the University of Campinas (Unicamp) and studied at the Apple Developer Academy, which first brought him into the mobile world in 2017. Before joining Nu, he spent three years working with iOS at Movile and iFood, and in 2018 created a World Cup sticker-album app with friends that reached the top-3 downloads in the Brazilian app store for several weeks during the 2018 World Cup.
Outside of work, Rodrigo enjoys playing and following soccer, traveling, and playing computer games—especially Valorant.

sessions.name: Scaling to 135+ million customers at Nu

bio: Rosário P. Fernandes (@thatfiredev) is a Developer Relations Engineer on the Firebase team at Google, focused on helping developers integrate Firebase and Generative AI into their mobile apps. With a passion for developer communities, he can often be found hanging out with other Android, Firebase, or Flutter developers. In his free-time, he enjoys playing videogames, reading comic books and watching movies.

sessions.name: Full Stack AI features

bio: I’m a Senior Engineer at Very Good Ventures with over a decade of experience in mobile development and more than four years dedicated to scaling the Flutter and Dart ecosystem. For the past few months, I've been working on producing enterprise-level and open-source code within a fully AI-assisted environment.
tagLine: Senior Engineer II at Very Good Ventures

bio: Stacy leads the Fanatics App and client platforms in the Fanatics Ecosystem. She is a multifaceted, international keynote speaker noted for her work in diversity advocacy, tech democracy, and "real-talk" leadership development. She has been a mobile specialist her whole career and worked on nearly every aspect of the ecosystem, currently specializing in engaging, fan-first Sports experiences for Fanatics. Her work has been featured on sites like Wired, Gizmodo, and Geek.com in addition to being mentioned directly in published books and being named to WWC's "100 Technologists to Watch". You can catch Stacy posting advice on LinkedIn, traveling to conferences, or working in her local DFW community as an educator and mentor for all career stages.

sessions.name: Congratulations, You're Staff. Now Why Are You Still Stuck?

bio:
Steven Stamps is an Executive Thought Leader and the architect of the VCoE™ Governance Framework for Google's Antigravity agent orchestration architecture. The Flutter & GCP reference implementation is designed to move software development beyond human-assisted AI toward autonomous, agent-led execution.

With a career spanning over four decades at the highest levels of engineering leadership, Steven has served as a Senior Executive at MGM Resorts International and The Walt Disney Company, where he managed high-scale human teams and led complex digital transformations.

Today, Steven utilizes a 24-agent autonomous workforce—built on Google Antigravity and Gemini Pro—to to help startups and new internal corporate initiatives quickly journey from product intent to Flutter and GCP deployment.

An instrument-rated private pilot and an avid collector of precision automobiles, Steven brings a pilot’s discipline and an executive’s strategic lens to the world of Agentic Software Engineering (ASE). His work focuses on the "Sovereign" intersection of product management, high-fidelity Flutter architecture based on GCP/Firebase, and the automated orchestration of specialized AI personas.

sessions.name: Next-level Generative AI ::: Moving Beyond Copilots to a Multi-Agent Autonomous Workforce (Roundtabl
Next-level Generative AI ::: Moving Beyond Copilots to a Multi-Agent Autonomous Workforce

bio: Taaha Rauf is an Senior Engineer at Very Good Ventures with five years of experience in Flutter development. His background includes contributions to both early-stage ventures and established enterprises. He has experience across the full lifecycle of app development, from initial creation to integration within cross-functional teams.
tagLine: Senior Software Engineer at Very Good Ventures

sessions.name: Auth flows in Flutter WebViews without losing your mind (or your users' sessions)

bio: I am a mobile engineer with 15 years of experience, with a focus on enterprise scale applications. Nowadays I make Flutter mobile apps, websites, and desktop applications for Universal's suite of parks and experiences.
tagLine: Principal Software Engineer at Universal Destinations and Experiences

sessions.name: The Flutter Enterprise Journey: From Native Apps to AI-Enabled Multi-Platform

bio:
Troy Meek is Vice President of Mobile, Web, and Commerce Engineering & Operations at Universal Destinations & Experiences, where he leads the engineering, platform operations, and digital delivery teams responsible for powering guest experiences across web, mobile, and commerce platforms. In this role, Troy oversees the technology that supports millions of annual guest interactions across Universal Orlando Resort, Universal Studios Hollywood, Universal Studios Japan, Universal Kids Resort, and Universal Horror unleashed.

With more than a decade at Universal, Troy has led large-scale digital transformations, global platform modernization initiatives, high-volume commerce systems, and enterprise mobile application delivery. He is passionate about building high-performing engineering teams, driving operational excellence, and leveraging modern technologies to create seamless customer experiences at scale.

At FlutterCon 2026, Troy shares practical insights from leading enterprise mobile engineering organizations and implementing Flutter in complex, mission-critical environments where performance, reliability, and business outcomes matter.
tagLine: NBCUniversal, VP Global Technology & Innovation – Web, Mobile & Commerce

sessions.name: Multi-Platform Strategic Advantage @ Scale: Flutter at UDX and Sky

bio: Tyler Stromberg is a Principal Software Engineer at SoFi with more than a decade of experience building and scaling mobile applications across fintech, transportation, and consumer technology. Before SoFi, he held engineering roles at Cash App, Uber and AKQA, where he contributed to mobile security, platform architecture, and high-impact consumer app experiences for brands including Delta Airlines and Audi. Tyler brings a practical, systems-minded perspective to modern app development and enjoys sharing lessons from building releable mobile products at scale.

I genuinely enjoy being a part of the Flutter community and am always eager to help others and share what I've learned. I have previously presented at community events, such as FlutterCon USA 2024 and 2025. Excited to connect with fellow developers and learn together!
